events.title: Number of executions in North Korea rose dramatically during Covid – report
events.description: Regime used its isolation after closing borders to escalate killings when global scrutiny disappeared, NGO claims North Korea dramatically increased its use of the death penalty after closing its borders during the Covid-19 pandemic, using its isolation to escalate killings when international scrutiny disappeared, according to a report mapping 13 years of executions under the country’s leader, Kim Jong-un . The number of documented cases of executions and death sentences increased by 117% in the nearly five years after North Korea sealed its borders in January 2020 compared with an equal period before the closure, according to a report by the Transitional Justice Working Group (TJWG), a human rights NGO in Seoul. Continue reading...

events.title: Bomb blast on Colombia highway leaves 21 dead amid pre-election violence
events.description: Cocaine-trafficking rebels blamed for worst attack on civilians in decades, which also left 56 people injured The death toll in a Colombian highway bombing blamed on cocaine-trafficking rebels has risen to 21, the government said on Monday, in the country’s worst attack on civilians in decades and just ahead of elections. The attack on Saturday left 56 injured and buses and vans mangled on the Pan-American Highway, in the restive south-western Cauca department. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Bomb blast on Colombia highway leaves 21 dead amid pre-election violence

events.title: Gunmen kill at least 29 at football pitch in north-east Nigeria, governor says
events.description: Attack in Adamawa state continues wave of violence across the country, including armed raid on orphanage in Kogi Gunmen have killed at least 29 people in north-east Nigeria, a state governor said on Monday, with local people saying the attackers targeted young people gathered at a football pitch, the latest bout of deadly unrest in Africa’s most populous nation. The attack on Sunday occurred in Adamawa state, which borders Cameroon, and is a hotspot for violence by jihadists and criminal gangs. Communal violence over conflict for land is also rife in the state. Continue reading...

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 April: Everyone’s paying the price for Hormuz closure, Guterres warns Security Council
events.description: The recently extended ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon remains under strain as violence continues, with civilian deaths and injuries reported in Israeli strikes over the weekend. Meanwhile, the Security Council is debating maritime security with the UN chief calling on nations restricting ship movement through the Strait of Hormuz to “let the global economy breathe.” Follow in-depth meeting coverage here. Stay with us for live updates.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 April: Everyone’s paying the price for Hormuz closure, Guterres warns Security Council

events.title: Chokepoints and conflict: How the Hormuz crisis is exposing global shipping vulnerabilities
events.description: The blockading of ships in the Strait of Hormuz as a result of the conflict between the United States and Iran has demonstrated how ships and seafarers have become “leverage in geopolitical disputes,” according to the head of the UN’s International Maritime Organization (IMO).

events.title: Brittany Higgins criticises federal inaction as Victoria moves to protect victim-survivors’ counselling records
events.description: Exclusive: State government commits to strengthening laws as Higgins labels Albanese government’s response to women’s safety issues ‘disheartening’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Victorian government will strengthen laws regarding the use of victim-survivors’ confidential communications after a push by advocates including Brittany Higgins, who described her experience of having counselling records subpoenaed as a “violation”. In an interview with Guardian Australia, Higgins was also critical of the federal government’s lack of action following a sweeping review into the justice system’s responses to sexual violence, saying it had “completely fallen off the agenda”. Information and support for anyone affected by rape or sexual abuse issues is available from the following organisations. In Australia, support is available at 1800Respect (1800 737 732). In the UK, Rape Crisis offers support on 0808 500 2222. In the US, Rainn offers support on 800-656-4673. Other international helplines can be found at ibiblio.org/rcip/internl.html Continue reading...

events.title: Darfur: Two decades on, a new generation of children faces 'horrific violence'
events.description: Twenty years after the conflict in Darfur first sparked global outrage, children in the region are once again trapped in a catastrophic cycle of violence, hunger, and displacement – but this time, the world is failing to take notice.

events.title: Two-thirds of global hunger concentrated in 10 conflict-hit countries
events.description: A growing share of global hunger is becoming entrenched in a small group of conflict-hit countries, with two-thirds of people facing acute food insecurity concentrated in just 10 nations, a major international report backed by UN agencies warns.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Two-thirds of global hunger concentrated in 10 conflict-hit countries

events.title: US strike kills three on alleged narco boat as campaign death toll hits 185
events.description: Military video shows boat moving swiftly in water before explosion leaves it in flames The US military said on Sunday three men were killed when it struck a boat it claimed was “engaged in narco-trafficking operations” in the Eastern Pacific Ocean. This latest strike – which follows dozens of similar attacks on alleged drug boats in recent months – brings the US campaign’s death toll to at least 185, according to a tally compiled by Agence France-Presse. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US strike kills three on alleged narco boat as campaign death toll hits 185

events.title: Hamas influence looms over Gaza elections as experts warn vote could backfire
events.description: Hamas reportedly increased its hold in Gaza as Deir al-Balah holds its first elections in 20 years, raising concerns about terrorist group influence.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hamas influence looms over Gaza elections as experts warn vote could backfire

events.title: Train collision in Indonesia kills 14 as rescuers work to reach survivors
events.description: Efforts continue to free two trapped passengers in wreckage after long-distance train collides with commuter train outside Jakarta, injuring 81 The death toll from a train collision near the Indonesian capital Jakarta has risen to 14 with another 84 injured, the train operator said on Tuesday, as rescuers worked to extract survivors still trapped in the wreckage. The collision between a commuter train and a long-distance train happened late on Monday in Bekasi, just outside Jakarta. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Train collision in Indonesia kills 14 as rescuers work to reach survivors

events.title: Rising nuclear risks put global security pact to the test at UN Headquarters
events.description: At a moment of mounting nuclear danger and deepening global mistrust, governments from nearly every country have convened at United Nations Headquarters to confront a central question: will the world’s main barrier against the spread of nuclear weapons still hold?

events.title: World News in Brief: Airliner safety warning, drought in Somalia, solar boosts farming in Ethiopia
events.description: The head of the UN’s civil aviation agency, ICAO, has called for immediate global action to protect commercial flights from escalating military threats, including missiles, drones, and GPS signal jamming.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Airliner safety warning, drought in Somalia, solar boosts farming in Ethiopia

events.title: Rescuers race to free survivors trapped inside train after fatal Indonesia crash
events.description: Two trains collided on the outskirts of the Indonesian capital on Monday, killing at least 15 people.

events.title: World leaders condemn ‘unacceptable’ violence after armed attack disrupts WH Correspondents’ Dinner
events.description: Global leaders expressed relief that Trump was unharmed after an armed suspect allegedly attacked the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington.

events.title: Fears of resurgence in Somali piracy after three vessels hijacked in a week
events.description: Pirates appear to be taking advantage of international naval strength being diverted to Middle East Three vessels have been hijacked off the coast of Somalia in the past week, raising fears of a resurgence in piracy around the Horn of Africa, and adding to the woes of the global shipping industry. The merchant vessel Sward was taken over on 26 April, a day after a dhow was seized. These followed the 21 April hijacking of Honour 25, a motor tanker carrying 18,000 barrels of oil, according to the Maritime Security Centre Indian Ocean (MSCIO), the tracking service of the EU’s naval force. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fears of resurgence in Somali piracy after three vessels hijacked in a week

events.title: Mexico warns US involvement in anti-drug operation should not be repeated
events.description: Claudia Sheinbaum says Mexico was not aware of US participation until four officials were killed in car crash Claudia Sheinbaum , Mexico’s president, said on Monday that her government told the United States, in a diplomatic note, that the unauthorized presence of US officials at an anti-narcotics operation in the northern state of Chihuahua should not be repeated. The incident came to light after two US officials, along with two Mexican officials, were killed in a car crash on 19 April after the operation. Sheinbaum has said the federal government was not aware of the participation of the US officials, who were widely reported to be CIA officers. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mexico warns US involvement in anti-drug operation should not be repeated

events.title: At least 14 dead in Indonesia train crash
events.description: At least 14 people have been killed in a train collision in western Indonesia, with another 84 injured, according to the country's train operator.

events.title: Central Sahel: Millions of children in humanitarian need, UNICEF official warns
events.description: Nearly 7.5 million children across the Central Sahel region in Africa are in urgent need of humanitarian assistance – “an emergency that remains too far from the attention of the international community,” a senior official with the UN child rights agency UNICEF has said.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Central Sahel: Millions of children in humanitarian need, UNICEF official warns

events.title: World News in Brief: Sudan drone attacks condemned, South Sudan violence, airstrikes in Ukraine, South Africa Freedom Day
events.description: The United Nations has condemned two recent drone attacks in Sudan, one of which left seven dead, Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric said on Monday during his regular media briefing in New York.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Sudan drone attacks condemned, South Sudan violence, airstrikes in Ukraine, South Africa Freedom Day

events.title: Trump admin warns Iraq over Iran terror proxies as US reportedly blocks cash payments
events.description: The Trump administration reportedly halted dollar shipments to Baghdad to pressure Iraq into disbanding Iran-backed militias that have attacked U.S. personnel.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump admin warns Iraq over Iran terror proxies as US reportedly blocks cash payments

events.title: Pakistan accused of attacking Afghan university
events.description: Air attacks on the province of Kunar have killed at least seven people and injured 75, sources tell the BBC.

events.title: Record bear sightings in Japan cause alarm as hibernation ends
events.description: Woman’s body found in Iwate prefecture last week, soon after a police officer was injured in bear attack nearby Rested but famished bears emerging from hibernation in Japan are already coming into contact with humans, with the pace of sightings outstripping that seen in 2025, a record year for bear attacks . According to media reports, the animals have been spotted with surprising frequency in urban areas in the country’s north-east, with authorities urging caution among people planning to spend the coming Golden Week public holidays in the countryside. Continue reading...

events.title: Deadlock over Iran's nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz cripples peace efforts
events.description: Two months after the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran started the war, peace talks are on hold, with control of the Strait of Hormuz and the future of Iran's nuclear program as the two main points of contention.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Deadlock over Iran's nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz cripples peace efforts

events.description: Officials say 20 people died after an explosive device detonated on a bus in southwestern Colombia's Cauca region, injuring 36 others Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Death toll from Colombia bus bombing rises to 20 during wave of violence

events.title: Rescuers recover last victims from Indonesia train wreck that killed 14
events.description: Rescuers have finished removing victims from a damaged commuter train car, confirming the crash outside Indonesia’s capital killed 14 people

events.title: Mali PM urges 'not to panic' after coordinated attack by jihadists, separatists
events.description: An alliance of al-Qaida-linked militants and separatists carried out the largest coordinated attack in over a decade in Mali, marking a dangerous escalation in what is widely considered one of the world’s deadliest regions for extremist violence.

events.title: Mali: Guterres calls for international solutions to curb spread of violent extremism in the Sahel
events.description: There are reports of continuing clashes in Mali on Sunday, a day after a series of coordinated attacks across the landlocked African nation against Government forces by extremists and northern separatist rebels.

events.title: South Pacific islanders strive to rescue their home from toxic legacy
events.description: More than 80 years after World War Two, the Solomon Islands remain one of the most heavily mine-contaminated places in the Pacific.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 South Pacific islanders strive to rescue their home from toxic legacy

events.title: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
events.description: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack

events.title: Violence escalates in Colombia with dozens of attacks before presidential vote
events.description: A spate of attacks against civilians and military bases in Colombia's southwestern region has raised security concerns as the country heads to a May presidential election.

events.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum rules out a conflict with US over 2 CIA agents killed in Chihuahua accident
events.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum has ruled out a conflict with the United States over an incident involving two CIA agents who died in Chihuahua

events.title: Nuclear experts warn Iran’s uranium ‘right’ is a myth, say Trump is right to hold firm

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Nuclear experts warn Iran’s uranium ‘right’ is a myth, say Trump is right to hold firm

events.title: Mexican special forces arrest top commander of cartel and his alleged money launderer
events.description: Arrests of Audias Flores and César Alejandro ‘N’ lead to gunmen blocking roads, as US embassy warns employees to avoid Reynosa after earlier arrest The Mexican authorities have arrested two top criminals, one of them a close ally of the slain founder of the Jalisco New Generation cartel (CJNG), prompting gunmen to block roads in the western state of Nayarit. Audias Flores, known as “El Jardinero”, is a regional commander in control of swathes of CJNG territory along Mexico’s Pacific coast. He was considered a potential successor to Nemesio Oseguera, alias “El Mencho”, who ran the cartel and was killed in a security operation in February . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mexican special forces arrest top commander of cartel and his alleged money launderer

events.title: UAE to quit global oil cartel OPEC, citing 'national interests'
events.description: The United Arab Emirates announced on Tuesday it will quit the global oil cartel OPEC and OPEC+ to focus on "national interests" as energy prices continue to climb amid the ongoing Iran conflict.

events.title: Third Ukrainian strike hits Russian oil refinery and prompts evacuations
events.description: Residents near a burning refinery in Tuapse are told to leave, and the Kremlin accuses Kyiv of destabilising global energy markets.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Third Ukrainian strike hits Russian oil refinery and prompts evacuations

events.title: Iran war: US wary of peace plan postponing nuclear deal
events.description: The White House has reportedly not ruled out a proposal from Iran to end its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz if the US lifts a blockade. However, US officials say any agreement must include a nuclear deal. DW has more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: US wary of peace plan postponing nuclear deal

events.title: Mali capital 'not under threat, but long term prospects do not look good', analyst says
events.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Dr Nathaniel Powell, West Africa Analyst at Oxford Analytica and Research Associate at Lancaster University's Centre for War and Diplomacy, explains that the jihadist group operating in Mali, JNIM, "does not appear to have the capability to seize and hold an urban area for any length of time, so Bamako is not yet under threat of being captured, but the long term prospects certainly do not look good".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mali capital 'not under threat, but long term prospects do not look good', analyst says

events.title: Dozens of Gaza children arrive in Jordan for medical care
events.description: A new group of wounded or ill Palestinian children from Gaza have arrived in Jordan to receive medical care, the kingdom's army said on Tuesday. They are the 26th group of children transported to Jordan since the start of the war in Gaza in October 2023. The 81 children, accompanied by 108 family members, arrived through the King Hussein, or Allenby, border crossing between the occupied West Bank and Jordan, the army said in a statement.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Dozens of Gaza children arrive in Jordan for medical care

events.title: 🇮🇩 A deadly train collision near Jakarta has left 15 people dead and 88 injured.
events.description: On Monday evening, a long-distance train slammed into a stationary commuter train that had stopped after striking a vehicle at an unmonitored level crossing. All 15 victims were women. 🗣️ "I thought I was going to die. We were trapped, but thank God, I made it out. It feels like a dream," said survivor Sausan Sarifah. Indonesia's president has ordered an investigation and called for tighter safety measures at level crossings.

events.title: Heavy flooding in southern China forces evacuations and leaves vehicles submerged
events.description: Heavy flooding from torrential rain submerged vehicles and forced the evacuation of more than 200 residents in a southern Chinese city
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Heavy flooding in southern China forces evacuations and leaves vehicles submerged

events.title: WHO calls for stepped up action to eliminate viral hepatitis
events.description: Countries are making measurable progress in combatting viral hepatitis, but the disease remains a major global health challenge, the World Health Organization (WHO) said in a new report published on Tuesday.

events.title: What counts as the woods? Judge axes Nova Scotia’s ban that defied ‘commonsense definitions’
events.description: The court sided with a Canadian hiker who deliberately challenged the order imposed to curb spread of wildfires As wildfires raged across Nova Scotia last summer, the Canadian province made a simple plea to residents: stay away from the woods. As the situation deteriorated, authorities turned the request into a prohibition : anyone caught hiking under the shade of the forest canopy faced a C$25,000 fine – a figure more than half the average worker’s yearly salary. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ What counts as the woods? Judge axes Nova Scotia’s ban that defied ‘commonsense definitions’

events.title: Pompeii archaeologists use AI to reconstruct man killed in volcano's eruption
events.description: Archaeologists have used AI for the first time to digitally reconstruct the face of a man killed in the AD 79 eruption of Mount Vesuvius, offering a new way to understand one of history's most famous natural disasters.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Pompeii archaeologists use AI to reconstruct man killed in volcano's eruption

events.title: Austrian man admits plot to attack 2024 Taylor Swift concert
events.description: An Austrian citizen accused of planning an attack at one of Taylor Swift's concerts in Vienna in 2024 pleaded guilty as his trial opened on Tuesday. The man was also tried on various terrorism charges.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Austrian man admits plot to attack 2024 Taylor Swift concert

events.title: Man pleads guilty over terror plot to attack Taylor Swift concert
events.description: A man accused of plotting to attack Taylor Swift concerts in Vienna has pleaded guilty on the opening day of his trial.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Man pleads guilty over terror plot to attack Taylor Swift concert

events.title: Czech court hands 7-year prison term to man over attempted synagogue arson attack
events.description: A Czech court has convicted a man of an attempted terror attack and attempted murder, sentencing him to seven years in prison
events.ticker_label: 💥 Czech court hands 7-year prison term to man over attempted synagogue arson attack

events.title: As warheads proliferate, decades old nuclear weapons treaty must evolve, warns UN chief
events.description: With top diplomats gathered at UN Headquarters to review the 1970 Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT), the UN Secretary-General warned that it must evolve to survive the age of AI and other new technologies.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 As warheads proliferate, decades old nuclear weapons treaty must evolve, warns UN chief

events.title: Iran’s good cop, bad cop game implodes as experts warn regime views US as 'evil'
events.description: Trump canceled planned Iran talks in Pakistan, citing regime infighting, and experts say Tehran's good cop, bad cop negotiating tactic has collapsed.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran’s good cop, bad cop game implodes as experts warn regime views US as 'evil'

events.title: Colombia climate conference highlights lack of financing for shift from fossil fuels
events.description: Lack of financing is one of the biggest barriers to moving away from fossil fuels, officials and experts said at a global conference Monday in Colombia aimed at speeding up the shift from fossil fuels to cleaner energy. The gathering in the Caribbean city of Santa Marta comes as governments face mounting pressure to move beyond climate pledges and begin outlining how to phase out oil, gas and coal, the main drivers of global warming. While U.N. climate talks have acknowledged the need for a transition, they have produced few concrete mechanisms, leaving countries and regions to grapple with the economic challenges largely on their own.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Colombia climate conference highlights lack of financing for shift from fossil fuels

events.title: From translating Agatha Christie at 17 to redefining Nordic Noir: Ragnar Jónasson's rise
events.description: Before he became one of the leading voices of Nordic Noir, Ragnar Jónasson was a teenager who translated novels by Agatha Christie into Icelandic. That early immersion in the mechanics of crime fiction helped shape a writer now published in around 40 countries, with millions of copies sold worldwide and a particularly devoted readership in France.

events.title: Officials say the death toll in a train crash outside Indonesia's capital has risen to 14
events.description: Officials say the death toll in a train crash outside Indonesia's capital has risen to 14
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Officials say the death toll in a train crash outside Indonesia's capital has risen to 14

events.title: Brooklyn attack leaves 3 injured, suspect wearing Iranian flag shirt arrested by NYPD
events.description: A suspect in an Iranian flag shirt allegedly assaulted three Jewish men in Brooklyn while screaming hateful language at them, according to reports.

events.title: 89-year-old man allegedly opens fire with shotgun in Greek capital, injuring 4
events.description: An 89-year-old man allegedly opened fire inside two Athens government buildings, wounding at least four people in a rare shooting incident in Greece.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 89-year-old man allegedly opens fire with shotgun in Greek capital, injuring 4

events.title: US political violence generates a familiar cycle - this time it's in overdrive
events.description: In modern America, it seems violence of this kind has become an ever-present storm that can strike anywhere and at any moment.

events.ticker_label: 🪖 FIFA cut World Cup ref following arrest for alleged sexual assault in UK

events.title: Taking power in Mali might be a stretch but insurgents can force hand of weakened regime
events.description: Coordinated attack by JNIM and the Tuareg minority inflicted significant casualties on government forces and Russian auxiliaries When al-Qaida-affiliated Islamic militants launched a series of attacks on military bases and raids into major towns in Mali and neighbouring Burkina Faso last summer, observers suggested they had been inspired by their counterparts in Syria, who had overthrown the regime of Bashar al-Assad and taken power six months or so earlier. Despite the tactical successes that earned them the fearful title of the “Ghost Army”, seizing swathes of territory and denying cities and the military of fuel and other essentials, the chances of Jama’at Nusrat ul-Islam wa al-Muslimin (JNIM) definitively defeating Mali’s military regime and the thousand or so Russian mercenaries hired to defend it looked poor. Continue reading...

events.title: Alleged Correspondents' Dinner shooter to appear in court. And, Charles III visits U.S.
events.description: The suspect in the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting incident is set to appear in federal court today. And, King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrive in Washington today for a state visit.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Alleged Correspondents' Dinner shooter to appear in court. And, Charles III visits U.S.

events.title: UAE quits OPEC in shock move amid energy turmoil
events.description: The United Arab Emirates is withdrawing from the OPEC and OPEC+ oil cartels as the Iran war drives a historic energy shock and rattles the global economy.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UAE quits OPEC in shock move amid energy turmoil

events.title: Hezbollah-Israel ceasefire under strain as both sides trade fire
events.description: Israel's military on Tuesday warned residents of more than a dozen villages and towns in southern Lebanon to immediately evacuate and head northwards, saying Hezbollah's "violation of the ceasefire" was compelling it to act. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hezbollah-Israel ceasefire under strain as both sides trade fire

events.title: Renewables rising, Part 2: Seeking stability amid volatile fossil fuel markets
events.description: As global energy markets remain volatile and the price of oil high due to instability and conflict in the Middle East, more countries are looking at investing in renewables to shield their economies from the shocks caused by fossil fuel reliance, rein in import bills – and meet key climate action targets.

events.title: Suspected gunman aged 89 held in Greece after five wounded
events.description: Five people were left with non-life-threatening injuries, according to Greece's state broadcaster ERT.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Suspected gunman aged 89 held in Greece after five wounded

events.title: Performer describes locking eyes with Trump as they ducked for cover during shooting
events.description: Oz Pearlman - a self-described "mentalist" - was speaking to Trump, First Lady Melania Trump and press secretary Karoline Leavitt as the attack happened.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Performer describes locking eyes with Trump as they ducked for cover during shooting

events.title: Germany: Stranded whale 'Timmy' being transported towards ocean in special barge
events.description: Rescue teams are celebrating what they hope is a major win weeks into efforts to assist a stranded humpback whale. "Timmy" has been coaxed onto a special barge that will help steer him back towards the Atlantic.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Germany: Stranded whale 'Timmy' being transported towards ocean in special barge

events.title: French public media in the dock: Report led by far right ally raises concerns
events.description: A French parliamentary committee has voted to endorse a controversial report into the country's public broadcasting network, despite strong opposition from left-wing lawmakers. The report, drafted by Charles Alloncle – an ally of the far right National Rally – outlines sweeping reforms and cost cutting measures, including potential mergers of TV channels. Some MPs, however, say the report is politically biased and didn't want it to be made public out of fear of "legitimising" what they see as false claims about the media.

events.title: French parliamentary inquiry becomes far-right attack against public media
events.description: A routine French parliamentary inquiry into public broadcasters has turned into a high-profile political spectacle, amplifying far-right attacks on public media. Led by 32-year-old Charles Alloncle, the probe has drawn widespread attention through vociferous hearings and viral social media clips. Critics warn it risks undermining trust in public broadcasters, including FRANCE 24, and laying groundwork for the far right's pledge to privatise the sector if it wins power in 2027 elections.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 French parliamentary inquiry becomes far-right attack against public media

events.title: Man, 89, arrested after shootings leave several wounded in Athens
events.description: An 89-year-old man has been arrested after shootings in Athens left several people wounded.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Man, 89, arrested after shootings leave several wounded in Athens

events.title: Luigi Mangione 'does not support violent actions,' attorney says as political attacks mount across the US
events.description: Luigi Mangione's legal team urges end to political violence and distances accused assassin from suspects in similar but unrelated recent crimes.

events.title: Iran eyes revenge for Soleimani as WHCA Dinner shooting exposes security ‘vulnerability,’ expert warns
events.description: A former Pentagon intelligence officer claims the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner shooting exposed serious security vulnerabilities around Trump.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran eyes revenge for Soleimani as WHCA Dinner shooting exposes security ‘vulnerability,’ expert warns

events.title: Russian fighters confirm withdrawal from northern Mali city after separatist attacks
events.description: Ethnic Tuareg fighters say they are in control of Kidal following a weekend of nationwide attacks, alongside Islamist groups.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Russia claims its Africa Corps group prevented coup in Mali after rebels seize towns
events.description: Kremlin-controlled paramilitaries also alleged it inflicted ‘irreplaceable losses’ on insurgents avoiding civilian casualties Russia’s defence ministry has claimed its Africa Corps – the successor to the former Wagner mercenary group – had prevented a coup in Mali over the weekend, avoiding mass civilian casualties and inflicting “irreplaceable losses” on rebel insurgents. It said in a statement that its troops in the desert town of Kidal near the Algerian border had fought for more than 24 hours while completely surrounded and vastly outnumbered. It also alleged without providing evidence, that the militants had been trained by European mercenary instructors including Ukrainians. The casualty toll was not specified. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Russia claims its Africa Corps group prevented coup in Mali after rebels seize towns

events.title: Evacuees from flooded remote Indigenous areas in NT housed in compound likened to ‘a prison camp’
events.description: Residents must sign in and out at a security gate, and vehicles and bags are routinely searched Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Hundreds of evacuees from remote Indigenous communities in the Northern Territory have been housed behind temporary fences and denied visitors after being forced to evacuate their homes in the most vicious wet season on record. In March, the Daly River in the NT reached a record peak of 23.93 metres, forcing families from Palumpa and Nauiyu to flee for the second time in four weeks. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Evacuees from flooded remote Indigenous areas in NT housed in compound likened to ‘a prison camp’

events.title: Lightning strikes in Bangladesh kill 14 - including 10-year-old boy
events.description: A 10-year-old boy is reported to have been killed in lightning strikes which left at least 14 people dead in Bangladesh.

events.title: Lithuania says it foiled an international plot to kill 2 activists

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Gaza in focus
events.description: The Security Council turns again to the Middle East crisis in New York on Tuesday, with scheduled debate expected to focus on the fragile Gaza ceasefire, a worsening humanitarian crisis there and efforts to advance a US-backed peace plan. UN Assistant Secretary-General for political affairs Khaled Khiari is set to brief, alongside former UK Prime Minister and member of the US-led Board of Peace Tony Blair. You can follow all the UN's meetings coverage here but follow live and in-depth updates below.

events.title: 5 bald eagles found dead in Michigan, raising poaching concerns

events.title: Working in France: Risky business?
events.description: How safe do you feel at work, both physically and psychologically? In this edition of Entre Nous we take a look at accidents in the workplace. We dive into what's behind France's status as one of the most dangerous places to work in Europe, and we also discuss how medical errors in French hospitals kill thousands of people every year. Finally, we dive into some of the solutions to making France safer on what is the UN's World Day for Safety and Health at Work and Workers' Memorial Day.

events.title: Guardian Essential poll: more Australians approve of Hanson’s party leadership than Albanese or Taylor’s

events.title: HSBC ‘reviewing’ private school perk for bankers in Hong Kong

events.title: French far-right 'definitely can' win 2027 presidential vote: Journalist Victor Mallet
events.description: "Far-right France" is the title of a panel discussion being held this Tuesday at the American Library in Paris. The panel will talk about the past, present and future of France's far-right movements, exactly one year head of crucial French presidential elections for which the far right is riding high in the polls. But can it actually go all the way and win? We put that question to one of the panelists, Victor Mallet, who is senior editor at the Financial Times and author of "Far-right France: Le Pen, Bardella and the Future of Europe".

events.title: Northern Beaches hospital handed to NSW government, ending troubled public-private partnership
events.description: Transition and legal handover of hospital from private operator Healthscope to NSW Health occurs at 7am on Wednesday Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Sydney’s Northern Beaches hospital is officially entering the public system, ending a troubled eight-year public-private partnership, although uncertainty about the future of private services remains. events.description: In January, Kim Keon Hee was sentenced to 20 months for accepting gifts from the Unification Church, which sought political favors.

events.title: China blocks Meta from acquiring AI startup Manus
events.description: Meta said Monday that the transaction "complied fully with applicable law" and that it anticipates "an appropriate resolution to the inquiry."
events.ticker_label: 💥 China blocks Meta from acquiring AI startup Manus

events.title: Iran's flurry of diplomacy continues in Russia, as Trump reviews Iran's latest proposal
events.description: Iran's foreign minister arrived in Russia on Monday, after a whirlwind weekend of diplomacy, seeking to gain political leverage and foreign backing as peace talks with the U.S. remain on hold.

events.title: Germany's birth rate reached lowest level on record in 2025
events.description: As the number of women of childbearing age declines in Germany, birth rates are projected to continue falling. The German Family Association says family-unfriendly structural policies are to blame.

events.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy rebukes Israel for buying grain 'stolen' by Russia
events.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has accused Israel of "purchasing stolen goods," namely grain from regions occupied by Russia. Ukraine summoned Israel's ambassador on Monday, and now it says another such ship has docked.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Ukraine's Zelenskyy rebukes Israel for buying grain 'stolen' by Russia

events.title: Nigeria's leader battles security, political crises
events.description: As politicians gear up for the 2027 elections, millions of Nigerians face a worsening security situation. Analysts say President Tinubu's recent cabinet reshuffle aims to rebuild public trust.

events.title: Japan looks to woo allies with new weapons deals
events.description: Tokyo has scrapped a decades-old ban on exporting lethal weapons, aiming to bolster allies as security tensions with China and North Korea intensify.

events.title: South Korea: Ex-first lady Kim handed longer graft sentence
events.description: Kim Keon Hee, wife of Yoon Suk Yeol, now faces a four-year jail sentence on charges of stock manipulation and bribery. The court also found she accepted gifts from a contentious church, knowing it expected favors.

events.title: Fact check: Cloud seeding didn't make it rain in Iran
events.description: Some social media users have claimed the alleged destruction of a climate research center in the United Arab Emirates caused intense precipitation in Iran. But those claims are misleading.

events.title: Bundesliga games abroad unlikely, but for how long?
events.description: New proposals from FIFA will allow domestic leagues to host one game abroad a season. As a result, Europe's football leagues could face a big change sooner rather than later.

events.title: India news: Google starts work on largest AI hub outside US in Vizag
events.description: Work has begun on a sprawling $15 billion AI hub in Visakhapatnam as India pushes to become a tech powerhouse. Meanwhile, West Bengal is getting ready for a second round of voting. DW has the latest.

events.title: Canada's Arctic noise is no party for narwhals
events.description: Known as the "unicorns of the sea," narwhals are fleeing Canada's far north. Researchers suspect a link to noise pollution from increasing ship traffic.

events.title: Hungary's Magyar seeks reset with Kyiv, proposes June summit with Zelensky
events.description: Hungary’s incoming prime minister, Peter Magyar, has extended an olive branch to Ukraine, proposing a June meeting with President Volodymyr Zelensky aimed at mending strained ties between the neighbouring countries and addressing longstanding disputes over the rights of Ukraine's ethnic Hungarians.

events.title: Paris Saint-Germain vs Bayern Munich: Champions League semi-final showdown
events.description: Titleholder Paris Saint-Germain hosts six-time winner Bayern Munich in the first leg of their Champions League semi-final on Tuesday. FRANCE 24's Sports Editor James Vasina tells us more.

events.title: Defending champion PSG faces 6-time winner Bayern in Champions League semifinal
events.description: Titleholder Paris Saint-Germain hosts six-time winner Bayern Munich in the first leg of their Champions League semifinal on Tuesday. Coach Luis Enrique's PSG is at full strength while Vincent Kompany's Bayern has already clinched a record-extending 35th Bundesliga championship and remains on course for a treble.

events.title: Comedian Kimmel hits back at Trump as tensions flare after Melania joke
events.description: US comedian and TV host Jimmy Kimmel hit back on Tuesday after Donald Trump called for his firing over a joke about Melania Trump, made days before an alleged assassination attempt on the president. Kimmel referred to the first lady as an "expectant widow", defending his remark as a playful jab at the couple’s age gap.

events.title: United Arab Emirates announces withdrawal from OPEC oil cartel effective May 1
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Cartel leader tipped to succeed 'El Mencho' captured
events.description: Mexico's army has captured a cartel leader tipped to succeed notorious drug lord "El Mencho".

events.title: True identity of woman's body revealed more than 20 years after being found by roadside
events.description: The true identity of a woman whose body was discovered in a rainwater collector has been revealed after more than 20 years.

events.title: Police seize suspected £4m drugs haul at port
events.description: A suspected £4m cannabis haul has been seized in a cross-border police operation.

events.title: Romania’s Socialists and a hard-right party seek to topple the center-right prime minister
events.description: Romania’s Social Democratic Party and the Alliance for the Unity of Romanians have submitted a no-confidence motion against Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan

events.title: Swiss court throws out graft case against imprisoned daughter of former Uzbek president
events.description: A Swiss court has thrown out the case against the daughter of a former president of Uzbekistan on charges including money laundering and bribery

events.title: Belarus frees prominent journalist Andrzej Poczobut in a 10-person prisoner swap
events.description: Officials say prominent journalist Andrzej Poczobut has been released from jail in Belarus in a swap with Poland that also saw a total of 10 people freed as the authoritarian leader of Belarus seeks improved relations with the West

events.title: Robot dogs with Musk and Zuckerberg heads roam Berlin gallery in Beeple's new exhibit
events.description: Robot dogs with hyper-realistic silicone heads modeled after famous figures like Elon Musk and Mark Zuckerberg are roaming a Berlin gallery

events.title: South Korean court convicts of wife of ousted President Yoon on corruption charges
events.description: An appeals court has raised to four years the corruption sentence for the wife of South Korea’s ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol

events.title: Uganda detains 231 foreigners in crackdown on possible human trafficking
events.description: Ugandan authorities say they have detained dozens of foreigners in a crackdown on illegal migration that was likely linked to human traffickers and cyberscam operations

events.title: UK leader Starmer faces more pressure over Mandelson ambassador appointment
events.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is facing new pressure over the decision to appoint Peter Mandelson as ambassador to Washington

events.title: A race for rights: How sport is helping protect girls in Uganda
events.description: On a red running track in eastern Uganda, coach Zuena Cheptoek is doing more than training runners. For many girls in the Sebei subregion, she is also a confidante, a mentor and first line of protection against female genital mutilation, child marriage and abuse.

events.title: For every generation, vaccines work and they have saved over 150 million lives: WHO
events.description: Over the past 50 years, vaccines have saved more than 150 million lives, as ordinary people chose to protect themselves, their children and their communities from diseases like measles, diphtheria, pertussis, polio, and rotavirus.

events.title: Affidavit in suburban Massachusetts mother's alleged slaying of children reveals gruesome details
events.description: A Wellesley, Massachusetts, mother is accused of murdering her two children and fleeing to Vermont, according to a newly released arrest affidavit.

events.title: Cold case cracked after 40 years as teen's alleged killers are caught after podcast helps find new leads
events.description: Four men were charged in the decades-old rape and murder of Roxanne Sharp after a podcast and advanced DNA technology revived the cold case.

events.title: King Charles set for historic address to Congress in first US visit as monarch and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Wisconsin teacher placed on leave after social media post advocating to 'make Americans great assassins again'
events.description: A Wisconsin teacher was placed on administrative leave after a controversial post suggesting he wanted to "make Americans great assassins again."

events.title: Nancy Guthrie's front door blood evidence points to ‘single abductor’: former FBI profiler
events.description: A former FBI profiler says blood spatter on Nancy Guthrie's porch indicates a single abductor took Savannah Guthrie's mother from her Tucson home.

events.title: Latin American leftists met in Spain, signaling push against US influence on continent
events.description: Analysts say the Barcelona summit led by Spain's Pedro Sánchez signals a progressive bloc forming to challenge U.S. influence in Latin America and beyond.

events.title: US targets Chinese refinery in sweeping Iran oil crackdown, sanctions ‘shadow fleet’ tankers
events.description: The OFAC sanctioned a major Chinese oil refinery and dozens of Iran-linked shadow fleet vessels in an escalation of a maximum pressure campaign on Tehran.

events.title: US economic chokehold on Iran reaches peak leverage as collapse risks emerge
events.description: U.S. economic pressure on Iran has reached historic levels, but inconsistent enforcement limits full impact of sanctions, a former Treasury expert says.

events.title: Iran escalates Hormuz 'tit-for-tat,' seizes ship tied to billionaire close to Trump, Macron
events.description: Analysts say the IRGC seized two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz in a tit-for-tat move after a U.S. naval blockade and seizure of an Iranian ship.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Iran escalates Hormuz 'tit-for-tat,' seizes ship tied to billionaire close to Trump, Macron
